title: Notebook re-canonicalizes path after read-tracker check (TOCTOU window)
description:
## Severity
Major

## Dimension
Security and Access / Concurrency

## Location
src/tools/file/notebook.rs:54-108 (`execute_notebook_edit`)

## Violation
The function does three filesystem resolutions and a read-tracker lookup in this order:

```rust
let resolved = match resolve_path(raw_path) { ... };       // line 54  - canonicalize #1
...
if !READ_TRACKER.has_been_read(&resolved) { ... }          // line 87  - check on `resolved`
...
let notebook_path = match std::fs::canonicalize(&resolved) // line 99  - canonicalize #2
    { Ok(canon) => canon.to_string_lossy().to_string(), ... };
...
if let Err(msg) = crate::guardrails::check_file_access(notebook_path) { ... }
let content = match fs::read_to_string(notebook_path) { ... };  // line 115
```

Between the read-tracker check on `resolved` (which is already a canonical PathBuf from `resolve_path`) and the second `canonicalize` on the same path, an attacker (or a sibling tool) can swap a symlink in the parent directory. The second canonicalize may then resolve to a different file than the one whose readability was attested. The read-tracker guard is bypassable.

Worse, `resolve_path` already canonicalizes (mod.rs:144-164). The second canonicalize is redundant — it exists only because the function was written without trusting its own helper. Use the already-canonical PathBuf throughout.

## Mandated refactor
Remove lines 99-108. After `let resolved = match resolve_path(raw_path) { ... }`, use `resolved.as_path()` everywhere. Pass `resolved.to_string_lossy().as_ref()` to `check_file_access` and `fs::read_to_string`. Single canonicalization eliminates the TOCTOU window and the duplicated work.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
